a:T5e74,<div class="specification">
<p>Three groups of human volunteers were given different amounts of almond nuts added to a controlled diet for a period of 68 days. During that period the amount of energy released in their feces was measured.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<p style="text-align: center;">[Source: Janet A Novotny, Sarah K Gebauer, David J Baer, Discrepancy between the Atwater factor predicted and<br/>empirically measured energy values of almonds in human diets, <em>The American Journal of Clinical Nutrition</em>,<br/>Volume <strong>96</strong>, Issue 2, August 2012, Pages 296–301, https://doi.org/10.3945/ajcn.112.035782]</p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p style="text-align: left;">Using the data, discuss the implications for the advice given by dieticians to patients.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p style="text-align: left;">Describe how the energy content of food may be measured by combustion.</p>
<div class="marks">[3]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p style="text-align: left;">State <strong>one</strong> material <strong>not</strong> produced by the human body that is egested from the digestive system.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">c.</div>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/biology/topic/d/","children":["← back to ","Biology"," topic ","d"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"19N.3.SL.TZ0.16"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Biology"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"SL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","3"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"19N"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","d-1-human-nutrition, d-2-digestion"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$a"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>a. increasing the mass of almonds eaten increases the energy content of feces ✔</p>\n<p>b. some of the energy consumed in foods may not be absorbed/is lost/egested in feces ✔</p>\n<p>c. can’t draw conclusions as the data is incomplete ✔</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>a. A calorimeter/calorimetry is used ✔</p>\n<p>b. food is burned/heated/combusted to release energy/heat ✔</p>\n<p>c. the energy/heat is used to heat up water ✔</p>\n<p>d. the rise in temperature of the water allows the energy in the food to be calculated ✔</p>\n<p>e. energy from the food = specific heat capacity of water x mass of water x temperature rise ✔ <em>Accept alternative correct formula</em></p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>cellulose/lignin/fibre ✔</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">c.</div>\n</div>"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">c.</div>\n</div>"}}]]}]]}]
